Defendant Lyft, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication As To Plaintiff Sabrina Marez'S Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-09-2018 Continued To Dec-10-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Chuck Geerhart, Esq. 415-498-2101 ext. 102 of Paoli Geerhart for Plaintiff Sabrina Marez.
Ilya Frangos, Esq. 650-345-8484 of Galine Frye Fitting & Frangos for Plaintiff Marissa Cruz
David Reidy, Esq. 415-844-9944 of McGuire Woods LLP for Defendant Lyft.
Lauren Koblitz, Esq. 415-362-2580 of Lewis Brisbois for Defendant Lyft.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff Cruz argues "coming and going" rule. Plaintiff Marez argues "car is the business". The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant Lyft, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication As To Plaintiff Sabrina Marez'S Complaint.

The Court rules in favor of Lyft on the list of issues that were briefed on all the coming and going issues and will not be revisiting any of the Le Elder discussions today. Furthermore, the matter is Continued to December 10, 2018 on the limited issue of "the business is the car"; Mr. Guarano was always in the course and scope when he was in the car. Plaintiff's supplemental Opposition is due on 10/31/18. Defendant's Supplemental Reply is due on 11/21/18.

Counsel for Lyft may submit a proposed order in coplaince with CCP 437(c)g and CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order To Postpone The Deposition Of Lyfts Person Most Qualified
Off Calendar Apr-30-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Lauren J. Koblitz, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois, T:415.438.5937, for defendant/moving party;
Chuck Geerhart, Esq. of Paoli Geerhart LLP, T:415.498.2101 x102, for plaintiff Sabrina Marez;
Ilya D. Frangos, Esq. of Galine Frye Fitting & Frangos, T:650.345.8484, for plaintiff Marissa Cruz.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling, modified, as follows:

DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'S Motion For Protective Order To Postpone The Deposition Of Lyft's Person Most Qualified is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendant Lyft, Inc. shall produce a person most qualified for deposition on or before June 15, 2018. Testimony shall be limited to (1) terms of contracts among Lyft, Hertz and persons renting vehicles in the Express Drive Program and information generally disseminated by Lyft about that program and (2) defendant Guarano's reporting of the accident to Lyft.

In addition, the Court continued the trial date to April 29, 2019. The hearings on defendant Lyft's motions for summary judgment are continued to September 28, 2018.

ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, email: mtorreano@sftc.org; torreanom@aol.com

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demand For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Jan-29-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Chuck Geerhart, Esq. of Paoli Geerhart LLP, T:415.498.2101 ext.102, for plaintiff/moving party;
Karyn N. Chandler, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois, T:415.438.6639, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF SABRINA MAREZ's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demand For Production Of Documents is granted in part. No later than February 16, 2018 defendant Lyft, Inc. must provide further verified responses without objections to the three document requests and produce responsive documents to the extent, and only to the extent, that those requests seek information about the terms of the contract or contracts between and among Hertz, Lyft and drivers/persons renting cars in the Express Drive Program including the contracts themselves and any information disseminated by Hertz and/or Lyft that is generally available to all drivers/persons renting cars in the Express Drive Program and/or those contemplating participating in the Program. Lyft is not required to provide any further responses regarding profitability, lack of profitability or economic/business analyses of the Express Drive Program.

Counsel for Ms. Marez is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.
